About: It is about the London Borough of Sutton and its four European twin towns.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Gary Drostle.
It is associated with Rob Turner.
BuiltIn: It was built in Sutton High Street.
ConsistsOf: It consists of seven individual paintings.
It consists of seven mock window frames.
CreatedBy: It was created by Gary Drostle and Rob Turner.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by scenes of the London Borough of Sutton.
It is characterized by scenes of Gagny a suburb of Paris in France.
It is characterized by scenes of Gladsaxe a suburb of Copenhagen in Denmark.
It is characterized by scenes of Minden in North Rhine-Westphalia in Germany.
It is characterized by scenes of Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf in Berlin in Germany.
It is characterized by the heraldic shield of each area.
It is characterized by a plant as a visual representation of its subject's environmental awareness.
It is characterized by a beech tree as a symbol of prosperity.
It is characterized by the London Borough of Sutton's most well known landmarks.
It is characterized by Gagny's Hôtel De Ville an ancient manor surrounded by a garden.
IsA: It is one of six works of public art in Sutton town centre.
It is a Victorian commercial building.
It is a friendship link with Tavarnelle.
LocatedIn: It is located in Sutton High Street.
It is located in the town of Sutton in Greater London, England.
It is located at the southern end of the High Street near the train station at the junction with Sutton Court Road.
PartOf: It is part of six works of public art in Sutton town centre.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Gary Drostle and Rob Turner.
UsedFor: It is used for telling people the story of the murals.
UsedIn: It is used in Sutton town centre.
